Investigating + Creating Performance Reports

Who?

Technical Support

What?

This SOP outlines gathering data about, troubleshooting, and creating performance reports.

Scope

Examples of performance issues

  • Slow loading times
  • Operations that lead to app freezing
  • Slow initial loads (new tabs, opening the desktop app, refreshing the page, logging in, etc.)
  • Slow page (route) changes (i.e., moving within the platform once loaded)


What is NOT 🙅🏾‍♂️ a performance issue?

Sometimes clients refer to these items as "performance" issues. Issues with the following features are bugs and should follow the standard bug reporting/escalating processes.

  • If you think the customer is demonstrating acceptable loading times, reach out to @performance-smes or the @performance-tim
  • Long wait time for Automations to fire
  • Long wait times for actions to occur from third-party integrations (Zapier, Make, Tray.io, etc.)

  • Data sync/refresh issues such as

    • Status not updating in another user's browser
    • Needing to refresh to see changes


(Feel free to raise to @performance-tim if user very frustrated/highly impacted)

When?

  1. Whenever a user writes in with complaints of slow loading or poor speeds
  2. After being pinged in a Social escalation
  3. Whenever a sales or red account escalation requests help with a client experiencing a performance issue


Reminders

  1. After confirming a performance issue, refrain from attaching a report to a defect in the standard way via the 650700028 (https://app.clickup-stg.com/333/v/li/650700028).
  2. If your user reports performance issues for a specific browser, desktop app, or mobile, you should still follow the process below and create a performance report inside the 980200129712 (https://app.clickup-stg.com/333/v/li/980200129712).


How?

🏅 Golden Rule

Screen recordings of the perceived performance issue(s) are most important.


If you have answers to the questions presented in the Speeds::Performance questions macro, it's time to create a report.


Here are some do's and don'ts when preparing a performance report.


Do

  • Ensure the issue(s) reported is not a functional issue/bug. (See "What is NOT a performance issue?" above.)
  • If you see the user encountering egregiously slow loading, do sympathize with them. If they're frustrated AND if you see clear signs of latency, you can acknowledge what they're experiencing is not up to our standard.


Don't

  • Indicate that their poor performance is our fault. Many performance issues are a result of poor hardware, internet connections, IT systems, etc. Do not admit/indicate for certain that the problem is with ClickUp.
  • Ask or suggest broad t-shooting steps e.g. check network connection, clear cache, try incognito etc.
  • Attempt to repro in user's Workspace. You won't be on the same network, machine, shard, etc. with them so a 1-1 repro is not possible.


1) Gather Data

  1. Select the Performance Issue checkbox in Zendesk and save the ticket:

  4. If you don't have the details of the performance issues, send the Troubleshooting::Slow Speeds::Performance questions macro, requesting critical data for the investigation.

    • ⛳️ NOTE FOR TS: If you're already assigned the ticket and you use the macro, Zendesk may un-assign you from the ticket. Be on the lookout and reassign yourself!
  5. Determine whether the user is truly experiencing performance issues.

2) Create a Performance Report

After confirming the user is experiencing one or more performance issues, gather the basic details and follow the steps below.


2a) Confirm whether a report already exists

Before spinning up a new Performance Report for a customer, confirm whether a report already exists

  1. Open this view Private (https://app.clickup-stg.com/333/v/l/ad-2564953)

  2. Search for the name of the Workspace or the user writing in

    1. If you see an existing and open report for that Workspace

      1. Create a new report through the intake Form using the customer's name as the title
      2. Open the new report and convert it to a subtask inside the existing report
    2. If you see an existing and closed report for that Workspace: Create a new report through the intake Form using the Workspace name and simply note the old report.


2b) Steps to create the report

  1. Open the CRM sidebar and select the vertical ellipsis to the left of the user's name.

  4. Select Performance Report Intake — a Form will open in a new browser tab.



  7. Complete the required fields in the form.

    • Use this Datadog query to pinpoint the city where most internet traffic originates. Replace the placeholder @userid value with your user's.

      • Use the city/state/province details with the most traffic, e.g.:

  8. Link the newly created Report with the existing Zendesk Ticket via the 'Link Existing' option in the CRM sidebar.



  11. Take action on any automated comments.
  12. Trigger the Private (https://app.clickup-stg.com/t/8x8uuy70n) macro and update it based on your conversation.
  13. Set the Issue Type field to the feature most closely aligned with your user's primary performance issue (e.g., if their primary issue is that loading List views is slow, use the Views > List > List issue type).

  16. Set the Resolution field to Bug > New > Performance.

    1. Set the Defect ID field to n/a.
    2. Set the Report ID field to the perf report ID.

  17. Send the TS::Bug Confirm::Performance Issues macro and solve the ticket. The performance report should be in Pending Investigation status unless otherwise requested by the @performance-tim.


HUB Tasks

When your report is created, we'll automatically check for a HUB task belonging to the user. If one can't be found, we'll ask you to search manually:


If you find a HUB task or notice one was applied:

  1. Link it to the defect with a standard task link.
  2. Stay extra alert of the report and reporter. If they are showing frustration or going unresponsive, please directly tag the AE of the account in both the Investigation Report and HUB task, outlining the current status of the investigation.
  3. If a Defect is eventually created, please link it to the HUB task and leave a comment in the preexisting thread.


FAQs

  • What do I do if I don't have all the required details for the Form submission?

    • If you need to submit an incomplete Form due to an escalation or other extenuating circumstance, feel free to use placeholder text in the required fields. Just be sure to supply the details later, as Eng relies on the information we provide!

  • What do we do when users report multiple performance issues but didn't share Clips of all the issues?

    • If the Clips the user did provide show clear performance issues (regardless of the feature area), create the report.

    • Otherwise, continue t-shooting with the user until:

      1. They share clear evidence of a performance issue; or
      2. You bug them up for a functional issue/bug.
